sprint.title: SEO v1: Full Public-Site SEO (Metadata + OpenGraph + Sitemap + robots.txt + llms.txt + Schema)
sprint.goal: Make Memorioso a strong SEO performer by adding complete metadata across all public pages, generating sitemap + robots.txt + llms.txt, and adding structured data where appropriate (Organization, WebSite, FAQPage). Ensure indexability is correct and no private/app routes are crawled.
definitionOfDone: Every public page has correct title/description/canonical/OG/Twitter metadata
Sitemap is generated and includes only public routes (with lastmod)
robots.txt exists, references sitemap, and blocks private/app routes
llms.txt exists at root and lists key public pages + description for LLM ingestion
Structured data (JSON-LD) added where relevant (Organization/WebSite + FAQPage on landing)
Noindex applied to non-public routes (auth, studio, account, book editor)
SEO smoke checks pass (meta visible in view-source, sitemap reachable, robots reachable)
Performance basics considered (no heavy scripts on public pages, no CLS regressions)

sourceNotes.robots: Google explains robots.txt purpose and limitations; it's used to manage crawler traffic and is not a security mechanism.
sourceNotes.sitemaps: Google recommends sitemap best practices and describes size limits and sitemap index patterns.
sourceNotes.faqSchema: Google documents FAQPage structured data and guidelines.
sourceNotes.llmsTxt: llms.txt is a proposed standard to help LLMs find site content; llmstxt.org describes the proposal and format.

backlog.title: Inventory public vs private routes and define SEO policy (index/noindex, canonical rules)

outputs: A route inventory table: public vs private
Index policy: which pages get indexed; which pages get noindex
rules: Each indexable page must define canonical URL
constraints: robots.txt should not be used as security (private content must still be protected by auth)

acceptanceTests: Route inventory exists and is referenced by sitemap/robots generation

backlog.title: Create SEO helpers (metadata factory + canonical builder + OG defaults)

constraints: No duplicated titles across key pages

acceptanceTests: All public pages can import and reuse metadata helpers
Canonical URLs are consistent and correct

backlog.title: Add/verify metadata on every public page (title, description, canonical, OG/Twitter)

constraints: OG image must exist and be reachable

acceptanceTests: Each public page has unique, relevant metadata

backlog.title: Generate sitemap.xml for public routes only (with lastmod)

implementation: Include only indexable public routes from lib/seo/routes.ts
constraints: Respect sitemap limits (50k URLs / 50MB) if you later add lots of pages

backlog.title: Create robots.txt (allow public, block private) + reference sitemap

implementation: Allow public assets needed for rendering
constraints: Robots is not security; private routes still need auth
Avoid blocking CSS/JS needed for rendering public pages

backlog.title: Add llms.txt at site root (LLM-friendly index of key pages)

format: Follow llms.txt proposal: short site description + list of key URLs
constraints: Keep it concise and maintained from a single config list

acceptanceTests: It lists only public URLs and describes the site accurately

backlog.title: Structured data (JSON-LD): Organization + WebSite + FAQPage (landing)

schemas: FAQPage for landing FAQ section (only if it’s real FAQ content users see)
constraints: Only add FAQPage if the page contains the FAQs (visible content)

acceptanceTests: Structured data is present in view-source
FAQPage markup matches visible FAQ items

backlog.title: Noindex enforcement for non-public routes (auth/app pages)

approach: Add robots meta 'noindex, nofollow' to auth/account/studio/book/billing routes
Optionally add X-Robots-Tag header for extra protection (route handler/middleware)
constraints: Do not accidentally noindex public marketing pages

backlog.title: SEO quality checklist + tooling docs (Search Console readiness)

optional: Prepare Google Search Console verification instructions

acceptanceTests: SEO checklist doc exists and is runnable by you

notes: Robots.txt is primarily for crawl management and isn’t a security mechanism. https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/robots/intro
Google documents sitemap best practices and limits. https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/sitemaps/build-sitemap
FAQPage structured data should match visible FAQ content. https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/faqpage
llms.txt is an emerging convention; llmstxt.org describes the proposal and format. https://llmstxt.org/
